rulings.Case Title: ANA MACDONALD VS. DUKE PARTNERS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Hearing On To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Joel C. Spann
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 20, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT NATIONSTAR MORTGAGE, LLC MOTION AND NOTICE OF HEARING ON TO HAVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION DEEMED ADMITTED; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES; DECLARATIONS OF JOEL C. SPANN is transferred to department 301 to be heard at 9:00 a.m. on January 4, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LOI TRUONG ET AL VS. XIAO ZHEN WU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Assignment Order And For Order Restraining Judgment Debtor; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 20, 2023 line 2. PLAINTIFF LOI TRUONG, MY NGUYEN, KEITH ARMSTRONG, SUMMER SIMPSON, KAROL NAVARRETE, LUIS HERNANDEZ MOTION FOR ASSIGNMENT ORDER AND FOR ORDER RESTRAINING JUDGMENT DEBTOR; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES is DENIED. The request for an assignment order not served in accordance with CCP 708.510(b) with no explanation or discussion of this provision in the points and authorities. The request for a temporary restraining order is DENIED. No authority is cited for the requested relief. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ERIC DEBBANE ET AL VS. CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 20, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, JOSE CISNEROS, BEN ROSENFELD AMENDED DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Petitioner states a claim for a declaration of validity of Proposition M. The facts giving rise to the tax have not yet occurred and the payment on such facts have not yet become due. Plaintiff's may obtain a declaration on essential validity of the tax without payment of the not yet accrued tax. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: KURT GRIMES VS. RUSHMORE LOAN MANAGEMENT SERVICES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 20, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT U.S. BANK TRUST NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, NOT IN ITS, RUSHMORE LOAN MANAGEMENT DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for violation of CC 2924(a)(1). Non-opposition filed. Demurer is sustained with leave to amend as to all other causes of action to address in good faith ALL issues raised by the demurrer. The Court notes that Plaintiff failed to participate in the mandatory meet and confer process and failed to address such failure. In the future, if Plaintiff fails to do so again, the Court will issue an OSC why counsel should not be held in contempt for failure to comply with the law. The Court further notes that the opposition, while making arguments and citing authorities, does not address most of the arguments raised in the demurrer. Furthermore, no courtesy copy of the opposition was received by the court. Failure to comply with SFLR 2.7B in the future will result in motions being treated as unopposed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
